545	  -- via `Module.Flat.isBaseChange`, then the Mayer-Vietoris descent.
546	  canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_affineCover sq F
547	    (fun V hV => canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen sq F V hV)
548	    U
549	
550	/-- **Flat base-change is an isomorphism** (Stacks tag 02KH, `i = 0`).
551	
552	The canonical base-change natural transformation `canonicalBaseChangeMap`
553	is an isomorphism at every coherent sheaf `F` under the hypotheses
554	`[QuasiCompact f]`, `[QuasiSeparated f]`, `[Flat g]`.
555	
556	The proof reduces section-wise via `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`
557	to the section-form helper `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso`,
558	which captures Stacks 02KH(ii) — the substantive algebraic content
559	(`Module.Flat.isBaseChange` on each affine open + Mayer-Vietoris for
560	quasi-separated `f`). -/
561	theorem canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
562	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
563	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g)
564	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]
565	    (F : X.Modules) :
566	    IsIso ((canonicalBaseChangeMap sq).app F) :=
567	  Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app.mpr
568	    (fun U => canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso sq F U)
569	
570	theorem flatBaseChangeCohomology {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
571	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
572	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g)
573	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]
574	    (F : X.Modules) :
575	    Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj
576	                ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj F) ≅
577	              (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f').obj
578	                ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F)) :=
579	  -- Build the canonical Beck-Chevalley base-change map and wrap it in `asIso`
580	  -- using the iso-claim from `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso`.
581	  ⟨@asIso _ _ _ _ _ (canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso sq F)⟩
582	
583	end AlgebraicGeometry
584

1	\chapter{The Quot scheme}
2	\label{chap:Picard_QuotScheme}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-174). This chapter is the A.2.b sub-row of Route~A
6	% (positive-genus arm of nonempty_jacobianWitness). It packages the
7	% Grothendieck--Altman--Kleiman Quot-scheme construction
8	% $\mathrm{Quot}^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ -- a projective $S$-scheme representing the
9	% functor of $T$-flat coherent quotients of $E_T$ on $X_T = X \times_S T$ with
10	% Hilbert polynomial $\Phi$ on every fiber. The Quot construction is the
11	% \emph{existence engine} behind the relative Picard functor: A.2.c
12	% (\cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}) wires this chapter together with
13	% the relative Picard functor (\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}, A.1.c)
14	% through the Abel map $\Div_{C/k} \to \Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}$, which factors
15	% through the Hilbert scheme $\Hilb_{C/k} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_C/C/k}$ -- the
16	% case $E = \mathcal{O}_X$ of the Quot functor. This chapter is gated on
17	% A.2.a (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}, written this iter) and
18	% A.1.a (\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}, on disk).
19	%
20	% LOAD-BEARING SUB-PREREQUISITE. The construction embeds Quot as a
21	% locally closed subfunctor of a Grassmannian
22	% $\mathrm{Grass}(W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V, \Phi(r))$.
23	% Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no Grassmannian \emph{scheme} -- only
24	% a linear-algebra Grassmannian as a finite-rank-quotient variety. The
25	% scheme-level Grassmannian over an arbitrary noetherian base is therefore an
26	% in-project sub-build. This chapter introduces it as
27	% \cref{def:grassmannian_scheme} and states its representability; it is a
28	% candidate for promotion to its own chapter
29	% \texttt{Picard\_GrassmannianScheme.tex} (writer iter-175+) -- see "Notes
30	% for Plan Agent" in the writer report.
31	
32	\section{Setup and motivation}
33	\label{sec:quot_setup}
34	
35	Throughout this chapter, let $S$ be a noetherian scheme, $\pi : X \to S$ a
36	projective morphism, $L$ a relatively very ample line bundle on $X$ over $S$,
37	and $E$ a coherent sheaf on $X$. The Quot construction packages the
38	$T$-flat coherent quotients $E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}$ of the
39	pull-back $E_T$ to $X_T = X \times_S T$, modulo the equivalence
40	$\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle = \langle \mathcal{F}', q'\rangle$ defined by
41	$\ker(q) = \ker(q')$. Fixing the Hilbert polynomial
42	$\Phi(\lambda) = \chi(\mathcal{F}|_{X_t}(\lambda)) \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]$
43	(constant on connected $T$ by flatness; see~\S\ref{sec:hilbert_polynomial})
44	stratifies the Quot functor into pieces $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$, each of
45	which Grothendieck proved is representable by a projective $S$-scheme.
46	
47	% SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1 and
48	% \S 5; FGA Explained Ch.~5, AMS MSM 123 (read from
49	% references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L287-L500 +
50	% L2154-L2542). The Hartshorne reference for the polynomial-eventually
51	% property of Euler characteristics is [Hartshorne], III.5.2 (the scanned
52	% PDF at references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf has no text layer, so
53	% the verbatim source-quote in \S\ref{sec:hilbert_polynomial} is taken from
54	% Nitsure \S 1; Hartshorne III.5.2 is cited in prose as the textbook
55	% reference).
56	
57	\section{Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf}
58	\label{sec:hilbert_polynomial}
59	
60	\begin{definition}\leanok
61	[Hilbert polynomial]
62	  \label{def:hilbert_polynomial}
63	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial}
64	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
65	  % Polynomials" (read from
66	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L478).
67	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
68	  % "Let $X$ be a finite type scheme over
69	  %  a field $k$, together with a line bundle $L$.
70	  %  Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
71	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
72	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
73	  %  the function
74	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
75	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
76	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
77	  %  coherence and properness conditions. The fact that $\chi(F(m))$
78	  %  is indeed a polynomial in $m$ under the above assumption is a special
79	  %  case of what is known as Snapper's Lemma (see Kleiman [K] for a proof).
80	  %
81	  %  Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
82	  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
83	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
84	  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$. Then for each $s\in S$,
85	  %  we get a polynomial ${\Phi}_s \in  \Q[\lambda]$
86	  %  which is the Hilbert polynomial of
87	  %  the restriction $\F_s = F|_{X_s}$ of $\F$ to the fiber $X_s$ over $s$,
88	  %  calculated with respect to the line bundle $L_s = L|_{X_s}$.
89	  %  If $\F$ is flat over $S$ then the function $s\mapsto {\Phi}_s$
90	  %  from the set of points of $S$ to the polynomial ring $\Q[\lambda]$ is
91	  %  known to be locally constant on $S$."
92	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); cf.\ [Hartshorne],
93	  III.5.2.}
94	  Let $X \to S$ be a finite-type morphism of noetherian schemes and let $L$
95	  be a line bundle on $X$. For a coherent sheaf $\mathcal{F}$ on $X$ whose
96	  schematic support is proper over $S$ and a point $s \in S$, the
97	  \emph{Hilbert polynomial of $\mathcal{F}$ at $s$ relative to $L$} is the
98	  polynomial $\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]$ characterised by
99	  \[
100	    \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}(m)
101	    \;=\; \chi(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}|_{X_s} \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
102	    \;=\; \sum_{i \ge 0} (-1)^i \dim_{\kappa(s)} H^i(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}|_{X_s} \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
103	  \]
104	  (a polynomial in $m$ by Snapper's Lemma; the sum is finite by coherence and
105	  properness). When $\mathcal{F}$ is $S$-flat the function
106	  $s \mapsto \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}$ is locally constant on $S$, so $\Phi$ is
107	  well-defined on connected components.
108	\end{definition}
109	
110	\section{The Quot functor}
111	\label{sec:quot_functor}
112	
113	\begin{definition}\leanok
114	[Quot functor]
115	  \label{def:quot_functor}
116	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor}
117	  \uses{def:hilbert_polynomial}
118	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "The Functors $\Hilb_{X/S}$ and
119	  % $\Quot_{E/X/S}$" (read from
120	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L394-L433
121	  % + L481-L498).
122	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
123	  % "Let $S$ be a noetherian scheme and let $X\to S$ be a finite type
124	  %  scheme over it. Let $E$ be a coherent sheaf on $X$.
125	  %  Let $Sch_S$ denote the category of all locally noetherian schemes
126	  %  over $S$. For any $T\to S$ in $Sch_S$, a \textbf{family of quotients of $E$
127	  %  parametrised by $T$} will mean
128	  %  a pair $(\F,q)$ consisting of
129	  %
130	  %  (i) a coherent sheaf $\F$ on $X_T = X\times_ST$
131	  %  such that the schematic support of $\F$ is proper over $T$
132	  %  and $\F$ is flat over $T$, together with
133	  %
134	  %  (ii) a surjective ${\mathcal O}_{X_T}$-linear homomorphism of sheaves
135	  %  $q:E_T  \to \F$ where $E_T$ is the pull-back of $E$ under
136	  %  the projection $X_T \to X$.
137	  %
138	  %  Two such families $(\F,q)$ and $(\F,q)$ parametrised by $T$ will
139	  %  be regarded as equivalent if $\ker(q) = \ker(q')$),
140	  %  and $\langle \F, q \rangle$ will denote
141	  %  an equivalence class. Then as properness and flatness are preserved
142	  %  by base-change, and as tensor-product is right exact,
143	  %  the pull-back of $\langle \F, q \rangle$ under an $S$-morphism $T'\to T$ is
144	  %  well-defined, which gives
145	  %  a set-valued contravariant functor
146	  %  $\Quot_{E/X/S} : Sch_S \to Sets$ under which
147	  %  $$T\mapsto  \{\mbox{ All } \langle \F, q \rangle \mbox{ parametrised by }T\}$$
148	  %  [...]
149	  %  This shows that the functor $\Quot_{E/X/S}$ naturally decomposes
150	  %  as a co-product
151	  %  $$\Quot_{E/X/S} = \coprod_{{\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]}\, \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}$$
152	  %  where for any polynomial ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$, the functor
153	  %  $\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}$ associates
154	  %  to any $T$ the set of all equivalence classes of
155	  %  families $\langle \F,q \rangle$ such that at each $t\in T$ the
156	  %  Hilbert polynomial of the restriction $\F_t$, calculated
157	  %  using the pull-back of $L$, is ${\Phi}$."
158	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
159	  Let $S$ be a noetherian scheme, $\pi : X \to S$ a finite-type morphism,
160	  $L$ a line bundle on $X$, $E$ a coherent sheaf on $X$, and
161	  $\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]$. The \emph{Quot functor with Hilbert
162	  polynomial $\Phi$}
163	  \[
164	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set}
165	  \]
166	  sends an $S$-scheme $T \to S$ to the set of equivalence classes
167	  $\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle$ of pairs $(\mathcal{F}, q)$ where
168	  \begin{enumerate}
169	    \item $\mathcal{F}$ is a coherent sheaf on $X_T = X \times_S T$ whose
170	      schematic support is proper over $T$ and which is flat over $T$;
171	    \item $q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}$ is a surjective
172	      $\mathcal{O}_{X_T}$-linear homomorphism, where $E_T$ is the pull-back
173	      of $E$ along $X_T \to X$;
174	    \item for every $t \in T$ the Hilbert polynomial of $\mathcal{F}|_{X_t}$
175	      with respect to $L|_{X_t}$ (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}) equals
176	      $\Phi$.
177	  \end{enumerate}
178	  Two pairs $(\mathcal{F}, q)$ and $(\mathcal{F}', q')$ are equivalent iff
179	  $\ker(q) = \ker(q')$. On morphisms $f : T' \to T$ over $S$, the functor
180	  acts by pulling back the quotient along $X_{T'} \to X_T$ (well-defined
181	  since tensor product is right-exact and preserves both flatness and
182	  properness). The Hilbert scheme is the special case
183	  $\Hilb^{\Phi,L}_{X/S} := \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}$.
184	\end{definition}
185	
186	\section{The Grassmannian scheme}
187	\label{sec:grassmannian_scheme}
188	
189	The Quot construction proceeds by embedding $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ as a
190	locally closed subfunctor of a Grassmannian over $S$. Since Mathlib carries
191	no Grassmannian \emph{scheme} (only a finite-rank-quotient variety in
192	linear algebra), we package the construction here.
193	
194	\begin{definition}\leanok
195	[Grassmannian scheme]
196	  \label{def:grassmannian_scheme}
197	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian}
198	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Elementary Examples, Exercises~(2)" and
199	  % "Construction of Grassmannian" (read from
200	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L546-L599
201	  % + L773-L850).
202	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
203	  % "For any integers $r\ge d\ge 1$, an explicit construction
204	  %  the Grassmannian scheme
205	  %  $\grass(r,d)$ over $\Z$, together with the tautological
206	  %  quotient $u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$ where \
207	  %  $\U$ is a rank $d$ locally free sheaf on $\grass(r,d)$,
208	  %  has been given at the end of this section. [...]
209	  %  Show that $\grass(r,d)$ together with the quotient
210	  %  $u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$ represents the
211	  %  contravariant functor
212	  %  $$\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$$
213	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any $T$ the
214	  %  set of all equivalence classes
215	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
216	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$.
217	  %  [...]
218	  %  Using the above show that, more generally, if $S$ is a scheme and
219	  %  $E$ is a locally free ${\mathcal O}_S$-module of rank $r$,
220	  %  the functor $\Grass(E,d)=\Quot_{E/S/S}^{d,{\mathcal O}_S}$
221	  %  on all $S$-schemes which
222	  %  by definition associates to any $T$
223	  %  the set of all equivalence classes $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients
224	  %  $q: E_T \to \F$ where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$,
225	  %  is representable. The representing scheme
226	  %  is denoted by $\grass(E,d)$ and is called the rank $d$
227	  %  relative Grassmannian of $E$ over $S$."
228	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2) and "Construction of
229	  Grassmannian" (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
230	  Let $S$ be a noetherian scheme and let $V$ be a locally free
231	  $\mathcal{O}_S$-module of rank $r$. For an integer $1 \le d \le r$, the
232	  \emph{Grassmannian of rank-$d$ quotients of $V$} is the functor
233	  \[
234	    \mathrm{Grass}(V, d) : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set},
235	    \qquad
236	    T \longmapsto \{ \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle :
237	    q : V_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F},\; \mathcal{F}
238	    \text{ locally free of rank } d \}
239	  \]
240	  with equivalence $\ker(q) = \ker(q')$. Concretely $\mathrm{Grass}(V, d) =
241	  \Quot^{d,\mathcal{O}_S}_{V/S/S}$ (the Quot functor for $X = S$, $E = V$,
242	  constant Hilbert polynomial $\Phi = d$).
243	\end{definition}
244	
245	\begin{theorem}\leanok
246	[Representability of the Grassmannian]
247	  \label{thm:grassmannian_representable}
248	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable}
249	  \uses{def:grassmannian_scheme}
250	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
251	  % affine patches" through "Projective embedding"
252	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
253	  % L798-L930).
254	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
255	  % "For any integers $r\ge d\ge 1$, the Grassmannian scheme
256	  %  $\grass(r,d)$ over $\Z$, together with the tautological
257	  %  quotient $u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$ where \
258	  %  $\U$ is a rank $d$ locally free sheaf on $\grass(r,d)$,
259	  %  can be explicitly constructed as follows. [...]
260	  %  the schemes
261	  %  $U^I$, as $I$ varies over all the
262	  %  ${r\choose d}$ different
263	  %  subsets of  $\{ 1,\ldots, r\}$ of cardinality $d$,
264	  %  can be glued together by the co-cycle $(\theta_{I,J})$ to form a
265	  %  finite-type scheme $\grass(r,d)$ over $\Z$. As each $U^I$ is
266	  %  isomorphic to $\AA^{d(r-d)}_{\Z}$, it follows that
267	  %  $\grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z$ is smooth of relative dimension $d(r-d)$.
268	  %  [...]
269	  %  We now show that $\pi : \grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z$ is proper. It is enough to
270	  %  verify the valuative criterion of properness for discrete valuation rings.
271	  %  [...]
272	  %  As $\U$ is given by the transition functions $g_{I,J}$ described above,
273	  %  the determinant line bundle
274	  %  $\det(\U)$ is given by the transition functions
275	  %  $\det(g_{I,J}) = 1/P^I_J \in GL_1(U^I_J)$.
276	  %  [...]
277	  %  We leave it to the reader to verify that the sections $\sigma_I$ form
278	  %  a linear system which is base point free and separates points
279	  %  relative to $\spec \Z$, and so gives an embedding of
280	  %  $\grass(r,d)$ into $\PP^m_{\Z}$ where $m = {r\choose d}-1$.
281	  %  This is a closed embedding by the properness of
282	  %  $\pi:\grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z$. In particular, $\det(\U)$
283	  %  is a relatively very ample line bundle on $\grass(r,d)$ over $\Z$."
284	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Construction of Grassmannian" (FGA
285	  Explained Ch.~5).}
286	  Let $S$ be a noetherian scheme, $V$ a locally free $\mathcal{O}_S$-module
287	  of rank $r$, and $1 \le d \le r$. The functor $\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)$ of
288	  \cref{def:grassmannian_scheme} is representable by a smooth projective
289	  $S$-scheme $\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d) \to S$ of relative dimension $d(r-d)$,
290	  equipped with a tautological quotient
291	  $\pi^* V \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}$ of rank $d$. The determinant
292	  line bundle $\det(\mathcal{U})$ is relatively very ample, and the
293	  associated linear system gives a closed embedding
294	  $\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d) \hookrightarrow
295	  \mathbb{P}_S\!\left(\bigwedge^d V\right)$ -- the \emph{Pl\"ucker embedding}.
296	\end{theorem}
297	
298	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the "Construction of Grassmannian" sub-section of
299	% [Nitsure], \S 1 (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
300	% L773-L930), which gives the construction in five named steps
301	% ("Construction by gluing together affine patches", "Separatedness",
302	% "Properness", "Universal quotient", "Projective embedding"). The total
303	% verbatim text is ~150 lines; it is reproduced piecewise in the
304	% SOURCE QUOTE comment of the theorem statement above.
305	\begin{proof}
306	  \uses{def:grassmannian_scheme}
307	  Over $S = \Spec \mathbb{Z}$ and $V = \mathcal{O}_S^{\oplus r}$ the
308	  Grassmannian $\mathrm{Gr}(r, d)$ is constructed by gluing
309	  $\binom{r}{d}$ affine charts $U^I = \Spec\mathbb{Z}[X^I]$ indexed by
310	  size-$d$ subsets $I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}$. The chart $U^I$
311	  carries the matrix $X^I$ of size $d \times r$ whose $I$-th $d \times d$
312	  minor is the identity, with the remaining $d(r-d)$ entries free
313	  variables (so $U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}$). The
314	  transition maps $\theta_{I,J} : U^I \cap U^J \to U^J \cap U^I$ defined
315	  by $X^J \mapsto (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I$ on the locus where $\det X^I_J$ is
316	  invertible satisfy the cocycle condition
317	  $\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{I,J} \circ \theta_{J,K}$. The glued
318	  $\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec\mathbb{Z}$ is smooth of relative dimension
319	  $d(r-d)$. Separatedness is checked from the diagonal
320	  $\Delta_{I,J} \subset U^I \times U^J$ cut out by $X^J_I X^I - X^J = 0$.
321	  Properness follows from the valuative criterion for DVRs: given
322	  $\varphi : \Spec K \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)$ over a DVR $R$ with fraction
323	  field $K$, factoring through some chart $U^I$ via a ring map
324	  $f : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to K$, choose $J$ minimising the valuation
325	  $\nu(f(P^I_J))$ of the minor; on the chart $U^J$ all entries have
326	  non-negative valuation, so $f$ extends uniquely to $\Spec R$.
327	
328	  The universal quotient $u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\oplus r}
329	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}$ is given on $U^I$ by the matrix $X^I$
330	  and glued by $g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in GL_d(U^I \cap U^J)$. The
331	  determinant line bundle $\det\mathcal{U}$ has transition functions
332	  $\det g_{I,J} = 1/P^I_J$; the global sections
333	  $\sigma_I \in \Gamma(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), \det\mathcal{U})$ defined by
334	  $\sigma_I|_{U^J} = P^J_I$ form a base-point-free linear system separating
335	  points over $\Spec\mathbb{Z}$, giving an embedding into
336	  $\mathbb{P}^{\binom{r}{d}-1}_{\mathbb{Z}}$, which is closed by
337	  properness. Representability of $\mathrm{Grass}(r,d)$ is then verified
338	  on the universal pair $(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), u)$: a quotient
339	  $q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\oplus r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}$ of
340	  rank $d$ on $T$ is everywhere locally trivialised; on the locus where
341	  the columns indexed by $I$ are a basis the matrix of $q$ has the form
342	  $X^I$, defining the classifying morphism $T \to U^I \subset
343	  \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)$. Base change from $\mathbb{Z}$ to a general noetherian
344	  base $S$ with a vector bundle $V$ is direct: trivialise $V$ on an open
345	  cover and glue. For non-trivial $V$ the same construction produces
346	  $\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)$ with Pl\"ucker embedding into
347	  $\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)$.
348	\end{proof}
349	
350	\section{Representability of the Quot scheme}
351	\label{sec:quot_representable}
352	
353	\begin{theorem}\leanok
354	[Representability of the Quot functor]
355	  \label{thm:quot_representable}
356	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme}
357	  \uses{def:quot_functor, def:grassmannian_scheme,
358	        thm:grassmannian_representable,
359	        thm:flattening_stratification_exists}
360	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, "Main Existence Theorems"
361	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
362	  % L2220-L2273); cf.\ Grothendieck, FGA TDTE-IV.
363	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
364	  % "Let $S$ be a noetherian scheme, $\pi: X\to S$ a projective morphism,
365	  %  and $L$ a relatively very ample line bundle on $X$. Then for any
366	  %  coherent ${\mathcal O}_X$-module $E$ and any polynomial $\Phi \in \Q[\lambda]$,
367	  %  the functor $\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}$ is representable
368	  %  by a projective $S$-scheme $\quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}$."
369	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, Theorem (Grothendieck), Theorem
370	  (Altman--Kleiman) (FGA Explained Ch.~5); cf.\ Grothendieck, FGA TDTE-IV.}
371	  Let $S$ be a noetherian scheme, $\pi : X \to S$ a projective morphism,
372	  $L$ a relatively very ample line bundle on $X$, $E$ a coherent
373	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-module, and $\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]$. Then the
374	  functor $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ of \cref{def:quot_functor} is
375	  representable by a projective $S$-scheme $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$,
376	  equipped with a universal quotient
377	  \[
378	    q^{\mathrm{univ}} :
379	    \pi^*_{\Quot} E \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}^{\mathrm{univ}}
380	    \quad \text{on } X \times_S \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S},
381	  \]
382	  flat over $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ with constant Hilbert polynomial
383	  $\Phi$ on every fiber. The Hilbert scheme is the special case
384	  $E = \mathcal{O}_X$: $\Hilb^{\Phi,L}_{X/S} = \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}$
385	  represents the functor of $T$-flat closed subschemes
386	  $Y \subset X_T$ with fiberwise Hilbert polynomial $\Phi$.
387	\end{theorem}
388	
389	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: this proof is long (~5 named sub-sections in [Nitsure]
390	% \S 5). Each sub-step is recorded with its own SOURCE QUOTE in the
391	% sub-lemmas below. The structural skeleton of the proof is restated in
392	% project notation in the \begin{proof} block.
393	\begin{proof}
394	  \uses{def:hilbert_polynomial, def:quot_functor, def:grassmannian_scheme,
395	        thm:grassmannian_representable,
396	        thm:flattening_stratification_exists,
397	        lem:quot_boundedness, lem:quot_alpha_injective,
398	        lem:quot_valuative_criterion}
399	  The proof has four steps; each step is a sub-lemma in the next
400	  sub-sections.
401	  \begin{enumerate}
402	    \item \textbf{(Boundedness, \cref{lem:quot_boundedness}.)} Pick the
403	      Castelnuovo--Mumford regularity bound $m = m(\mathrm{rank}\,V,
404	      \mathrm{rank}\,W, \Phi)$ uniformly across all fibers (by Mumford's
405	      $m$-regularity theorem applied to $E_s$ and to all coherent
406	      quotients of $E_s$ with Hilbert polynomial $\Phi$). For $r \ge m$
407	      and any $T \to S$ and any $T$-flat quotient
408	      $q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}$ with Hilbert polynomial
409	      $\Phi$, the higher direct images $R^i (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{F}(r)$
410	      and $R^i (\pi_T)_* E_T(r)$ and $R^i (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}(r)$
411	      vanish for $i \ge 1$ (where $\mathcal{G} = \ker q$), and the
412	      direct images $(\pi_T)_* \mathcal{F}(r)$, $(\pi_T)_* E_T(r)$,
413	      $(\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}(r)$ are locally free of ranks fixed by the
414	      data; the canonical maps $(\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_*(-)(r) \to (-)(r)$
415	      are surjective. Cohomology-and-base-change (\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology},
416	      Stacks tag 02KH) propagates these properties across $T$-base change.
417	    \item \textbf{(Grassmannian embedding, \cref{lem:quot_alpha_injective}.)}
418	      Reduce by \cref{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W} to the case
419	      $X = \mathbb{P}_S(V)$, $E = \pi^* W$ with $V, W$ locally free
420	      on $S$, $L = \mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{P}(V)}(1)$. Fix $r \ge m$.
421	      The Grassmannian embedding
422	      \[
423	        \alpha : \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} \longrightarrow
424	        \mathrm{Grass}\!\left(
425	          W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V,\; \Phi(r)
426	        \right)
427	      \]
428	      sends $\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle$ on $T$ to the locally free
429	      quotient $(\pi_T)_*(q(r)) : (\pi_T)_* E_T(r) \to (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{F}(r)$.
430	      This is injective on $T$-valued points: from the map $T \to
431	      \mathrm{Gr}_S(W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V, \Phi(r))$ one recovers
432	      the kernel-pull-back $(\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}(r) \to E_T(r)$,
433	      whose cokernel is $q(r) : E_T(r) \to \mathcal{F}(r)$;
434	      twisting back by $-r$ recovers $q$.
435	    \item \textbf{(Locally closed in the Grassmannian via flattening
436	      stratification.)} Let $T_0 = \mathrm{Gr}_S(W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V,
437	      \Phi(r))$ with universal quotient
438	      $f : (W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V)_{T_0} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{J}$
439	      and kernel $\mathcal{K}$. Form the composite
440	      $h : \pi_{T_0}^* \mathcal{K} \hookrightarrow
441	      \pi_{T_0}^* (W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V) = \pi_{T_0}^* (\pi_{T_0})_* E_{T_0}
442	      \twoheadrightarrow E_{T_0}$
443	      and let $q := \mathrm{coker}(h) : E_{T_0} \to \mathcal{F}$. By the
444	      flattening stratification (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}
445	      of \cref{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification}) applied to $\mathcal{F}$
446	      on $X_{T_0}$, there exists a locally closed subscheme
447	      $T_0^\Phi \subseteq T_0$ such that for any $\phi : Y \to T_0$, the
448	      pulled-back cokernel $\mathcal{F}_Y$ is $Y$-flat with fiberwise
449	      Hilbert polynomial $\Phi$ iff $\phi$ factors through $T_0^\Phi$.
450	      The pair $(T_0^\Phi, \text{universal cokernel})$ represents
451	      $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$.
452	    \item \textbf{(Closed, hence projective,
453	      \cref{lem:quot_valuative_criterion}.)} The functor
454	      $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ satisfies the valuative criterion of
455	      properness with respect to DVRs (Exercise (7) of \S 1, proved
456	      using that flatness over a DVR is torsion-freeness). Combined
457	      with the locally closed embedding of step 3 into the projective
458	      $\mathrm{Gr}_S \hookrightarrow \mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^{\Phi(r)}
459	      (W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V))$, this upgrades to a \emph{closed}
460	      embedding. Hence $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ is a projective $S$-scheme.
461	  \end{enumerate}
462	  Finally, by \cref{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W}, the general case
463	  (arbitrary $X$ projective over $S$ and arbitrary coherent $E$) follows
464	  from the case $X = \mathbb{P}(V)$, $E = \pi^* W$: pick a closed
465	  embedding $X \subset \mathbb{P}(V)$ and write $E$ as a coherent quotient
466	  of $\pi^*(W)(\nu)$ for some vector bundle $W$ on $S$ and integer $\nu$;
467	  the Quot scheme of the original data is then a closed subscheme of the
468	  Quot scheme of the universal data, by the closed-embedding clause of
469	  \cref{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W}.
470	\end{proof}
471	
472	\section{Sub-lemmas of the construction}
473	\label{sec:quot_sub_lemmas}
474	
475	\begin{lemma}[Reduction to the universal case]
476	  \label{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W}
477	  \uses{def:quot_functor}
478	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Reduction to the case of
479	  % $\Quot_{\pi^*W/\PP(V)/S}^{\Phi,L}$"
480	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
481	  % L2294-L2332).
482	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
483	  % "It is enough to prove Theorem \ref{Altman-Kleiman Quot} in the special case
484	  %  that $X = \PP(V)$ and
485	  %  $E= \pi^*(W)$ where $V$ and $W$ are vector bundles on $S$, as
486	  %  a consequence of the next lemma.
487	  %
488	  %  \begin{lemma}
489	  %  (i) Let $\nu$ be any integer.
490	  %  Then tensoring by $L^{\nu}$ gives an isomorphism of
491	  %  functors from $\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}$ to
492	  %  $\Quot_{E(\nu)/X/S}^{\Psi,L}$ where the polynomial
493	  %  $\Psi \in \Q[\lambda]$ is defined by $\Psi(\lambda) = \Phi(\lambda + \nu)$.
494	  %
495	  %  (ii) Let $\phi : E \to G$ be a surjective homomorphism of
496	  %  coherent sheaves on $X$. Then the corresponding natural transformation
497	  %  $\Quot_{G/X/S}^{\Phi,L} \to \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}$
498	  %  is a closed embedding.
499	  %  \end{lemma}"
500	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
501	  Let $S$, $X$, $L$, $E$, $\Phi$ be as in \cref{def:quot_functor}.
502	  \begin{enumerate}
503	    \item (Twist-shift.) For $\nu \in \mathbb{Z}$, tensoring by
504	      $L^{\otimes \nu}$ gives an isomorphism of functors
505	      $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} \xrightarrow{\sim}
506	      \Quot^{\Psi,L}_{E(\nu)/X/S}$ with $\Psi(\lambda) = \Phi(\lambda + \nu)$.
507	    \item (Surjection induces closed embedding.) Given a surjection
508	      $\phi : E \twoheadrightarrow G$ of coherent sheaves on $X$, the
509	      induced natural transformation
510	      $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{G/X/S} \to \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ is a closed
511	      embedding of functors.
512	  \end{enumerate}
513	  Consequently, if $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\pi^* W / \mathbb{P}(V)/S}$ is
514	  representable for all $V$, $W$ vector bundles on $S$, then so is
515	  $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ for every $X \subset \mathbb{P}(V)$ closed and
516	  every coherent $E$ on $X$ that is a quotient of $\pi^*(W)(\nu)|_X$ for
517	  some $W$ and $\nu$.
518	\end{lemma}
519	
520	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The statement (i) is obvious. The statement (ii) just
521	% says that given any locally noetherian scheme $T$ and a family
522	% $\langle \F,q\rangle \in \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(T)$, there exists
523	% a closed subscheme $T' \subset T$ with the following universal property:
524	% for any locally noetherian scheme $U$ and a morphism $f: U\to T$,
525	% the pulled back homomorphism of ${\mathcal O}_{X_U}$-modules $q_U : E_U \to \F_U$
526	% factors via the pulled back homomorphism $\phi_U : E_U \to G_U$
527	% if and only if $U \to T$ factors via $T'\hra T$.
528	% This is satisfied by taking $T'$ to be the vanishing scheme
529	% for the composite homomorphism $\ker(\phi) \hra  E \stackrel{q}{\to} \F$
530	% of coherent sheaves on $X_T$ (see Remark \ref{vanishing scheme}),
531	% which makes sense here as both $\ker(\phi)$ and $\F$ are coherent
532	% on $X_T$ and $\F$ is flat over $T$."
533	\begin{proof}
534	  \uses{def:quot_functor}
535	  (i) is direct from the definitions: tensoring with $L^{\otimes \nu}$ is
536	  an autoequivalence of coherent sheaves on $X$ (and on every $X_T$),
537	  preserving surjectivity, $T$-flatness, and properness of support, and
538	  shifting the Hilbert polynomial by $\nu$ in the variable.
539	
540	  (ii) Given a family $\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle$ on $T$ in
541	  $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$, the locus where $q$ factors through
542	  $\phi : E \twoheadrightarrow G$ is cut out by the vanishing of the
543	  composite $\ker(\phi)|_{X_T} \hookrightarrow E_T \xrightarrow{q}
544	  \mathcal{F}$. Because $\mathcal{F}$ is $T$-flat, the schematic image of
545	  this composite descends to a closed subscheme $T' \subset T$ such that
546	  $f : U \to T$ factors through $T'$ iff $q_U$ factors through $\phi_U$.
547	  Hence $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{G/X/S} \hookrightarrow \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$
548	  is a closed embedding of subfunctors.
549	\end{proof}
550	
551	\begin{lemma}[Uniform Castelnuovo--Mumford regularity bound]
552	  \label{lem:quot_boundedness}
553	  \uses{def:quot_functor, def:hilbert_polynomial}
554	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Use of $m$-Regularity"
555	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
556	  % L2346-L2404). Builds on [Nitsure] \S 2 (Castelnuovo--Mumford regularity)
557	  % and \S 3 (semi-continuity and base-change), cited internally as
558	  % Theorem~"Mumford" and Theorem~"Base-change for flat sheaves".
559	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
560	  % "We consider the sheaf $E = \pi^*(W)$ on $X=\PP(V)$ where
561	  %  $V$ is a vector bundle on $S$,
562	  %  and take $L = {\mathcal O}_{\PP(V)}(1)$.
563	  %  For any field $k$ and a $k$-valued point
564	  %  $s$ of $S$, we have an isomorphism
565	  %  $\PP(V)_s \simeq \PP^n_k$ where $n=\rank(V)-1$,
566	  %  and the restricted sheaf $E_s$ on $\PP(V)_s$ is isomorphic to
567	  %  $\oplus^p{\mathcal O}_{\PP(V)_s}$ where $p=\rank(W)$.
568	  %  It follows from Theorem \ref{Mumford}
569	  %  that given any $\Phi \in \Q[\lambda]$, there exists an integer
570	  %  $m$ which depends only on $\rank(V)$, $\rank(W)$ and $\Phi$,
571	  %  such that for any field $k$ and a $k$-valued point $s$ of $S$,
572	  %  the sheaf $E_s$ on $\PP(V)_s$ is $m$-regular, and for any
573	  %  coherent quotient $q: E_s \to \F$ on $\PP(V)_s$ with
574	  %  Hilbert polynomial $\Phi$, the sheaf $\F$ and the kernel sheaf
575	  %  $\G\subset E_s$ of $q$ are both $m$-regular."
576	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, "Use of $m$-Regularity" (FGA Explained
577	  Ch.~5).}
578	  Let $X = \mathbb{P}_S(V)$, $E = \pi^* W$ with $V$, $W$ vector bundles on
579	  $S$ of ranks $n+1$, $p$, $L = \mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{P}(V)}(1)$, and
580	  $\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]$. There exists an integer
581	  $m = m(n, p, \Phi)$, depending only on $n$, $p$, and $\Phi$, such that
582	  for every geometric point $s$ of $S$ and every coherent quotient
583	  $q : E_s \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}$ with Hilbert polynomial
584	  $\Phi$, the sheaves $E_s$, $\mathcal{F}$, and $\ker q$ on $X_s$ are all
585	  $m$-regular in the sense of Castelnuovo--Mumford. Consequently, for
586	  every $T \to S$ and every $T$-flat quotient
587	  $q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}$ on $X_T$ with Hilbert
588	  polynomial $\Phi$ and every $r \ge m$, the conclusions \textbf{(*)} and
589	  \textbf{(**)} of \cite[\S 5, "Use of $m$-Regularity"]{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
590	  hold: the direct images $(\pi_T)_* \mathcal{F}(r)$, $(\pi_T)_* E_T(r)$,
591	  $(\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}(r)$ are locally free of fixed ranks; the
592	  canonical surjections $(\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_*(-)(r) \twoheadrightarrow (-)(r)$
593	  hold; and the higher direct images $R^i (\pi_T)_* (-)(r)$ vanish for
594	  $i \ge 1$.
595	\end{lemma}
596	
597	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see [Nitsure], \S 5, "Use of $m$-Regularity"
598	% (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L2346-L2404).
599	% The proof invokes the m-regularity theorem of [Nitsure] \S 2
600	% (Theorem "Mumford") to extract the uniform bound m, and the
601	% base-change-for-flat-sheaves theorem of [Nitsure] \S 3 (cited as
602	% "Base-change for flat sheaves") to propagate the conclusions across $T$.
603	\begin{proof}
604	  \uses{def:hilbert_polynomial, thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}
605	  Each geometric fiber $E_s$ is a trivial bundle on $\mathbb{P}^n_{\kappa(s)}$
606	  of rank $p$, so $E_s$ is $0$-regular. The Castelnuovo--Mumford
607	  $m$-regularity theorem of [Nitsure] \S 2 produces, for any fixed Hilbert
608	  polynomial $\Phi$, a uniform integer $m = m(n, p, \Phi)$ above which
609	  every coherent quotient of $E_s$ with Hilbert polynomial $\Phi$ -- and
610	  its kernel -- is $m$-regular. The Castelnuovo Lemma of [Nitsure] \S 2
611	  converts $m$-regularity into vanishing of $H^i((-) (r))$ for $i \ge 1$
612	  and $r \ge m$, and into global generation of $H^0((-)(r))$. To
613	  propagate the fiber-wise statement to families over $T$, apply the
614	  flat-base-change theorem (\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}, Stacks
615	  tag 02KH) along $T \to S$: cohomology of flat families commutes with
616	  base change, so the direct images $(\pi_T)_*(-)(r)$ are locally free of
617	  the rank dictated by the fiber Hilbert polynomials, and the canonical
618	  maps $(\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_*(-)(r) \to (-)(r)$ inherit fiberwise
619	  surjectivity.
620	\end{proof}
621	
622	\begin{lemma}[Grassmannian embedding $\alpha$ is injective]
623	  \label{lem:quot_alpha_injective}
624	  \uses{def:quot_functor, def:grassmannian_scheme, lem:quot_boundedness}
625	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Embedding Quot into Grassmannian"
626	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
627	  % L2409-L2451).
628	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
629	  % "We now fix a positive integer $r$ such that $r\ge m$.
630	  %  Note that the rank of ${\pi_T}_*\F(r)$ is $\Phi(r)$
631	  %  and $\pi_*E(r) = W \otimes_{{\mathcal O}_S}\sym^r V$.
632	  %  Therefore the surjective homomorphism
633	  %  ${\pi_T}_*E_T(r) \to {\pi_T}_*\F(r)$
634	  %  defines an element of the set
635	  %  $\Grass( W \otimes_{{\mathcal O}_S}\sym^r V , \Phi(r))(T)$.
636	  %  We thus get a morphism of functors
637	  %  $$\alpha : \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} \to
638	  %  \Grass( W \otimes_{{\mathcal O}_S}\sym^r V, \Phi(r))$$
639	  %  It associates to $q: E_T \to \F$ the quotient
640	  %  ${\pi_T}_*(q(r)) : {\pi_T}_*E_T(r) \to {\pi_T}_*\F(r)$.
641	  %
642	  %  The above morphism $\alpha$ is injective because the quotient
643	  %  $q: E_T \to \F$ can be recovered from
644	  %  ${\pi_T}_*(q(r)) : {\pi_T}_*E_T(r) \to {\pi_T}_*\F(r)$
645	  %  as follows."
646	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, "Embedding Quot into Grassmannian"
647	  (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
648	  In the universal setup $X = \mathbb{P}_S(V)$, $E = \pi^* W$, fix
649	  $r \ge m$ from \cref{lem:quot_boundedness}. The natural transformation
650	  \[
651	    \alpha :
652	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} \longrightarrow
653	    \mathrm{Grass}\!\left(
654	      W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V,\; \Phi(r)
655	    \right),
656	    \qquad
657	    \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle \longmapsto
658	    \langle (\pi_T)_*\mathcal{F}(r),\, (\pi_T)_*(q(r))\rangle
659	  \]
660	  is well-defined (the target is locally free of rank $\Phi(r)$, and the
661	  canonical isomorphism $\pi_* E(r) \cong W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S}
662	  \mathrm{Sym}^r V$ gives the source) and \emph{injective} on $T$-points:
663	  the data of the locally free quotient at level $r$ determines
664	  $q : E_T \to \mathcal{F}$.
665	\end{lemma}
666	
667	\begin{proof}
668	  \uses{lem:quot_boundedness}
669	  Suppose $\alpha(T)$ sends both $\langle \mathcal{F}_1, q_1\rangle$ and
670	  $\langle \mathcal{F}_2, q_2\rangle$ to the same $T$-point of the
671	  Grassmannian. Then the pulled-back kernel
672	  $(\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}_i(r) \to (\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_* E_T(r)
673	  = \pi_{T_0}^* (W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V)_T$
674	  is the same. The diagram \textbf{(**)} of
675	  \cref{lem:quot_boundedness} gives a right-exact sequence
676	  \[
677	    (\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}_i(r) \xrightarrow{h}
678	    E_T(r) \xrightarrow{q_i(r)} \mathcal{F}_i(r) \to 0,
679	  \]
680	  so $q_i(r)$ is recovered as the cokernel of $h$; twisting by $-r$
681	  recovers $q_i$, giving $q_1 = q_2$ and hence
682	  $\langle \mathcal{F}_1, q_1\rangle = \langle \mathcal{F}_2, q_2\rangle$.
683	\end{proof}
684	
685	\begin{lemma}[Valuative criterion of properness for $\Quot$]
686	  \label{lem:quot_valuative_criterion}
687	  \uses{def:quot_functor}
688	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Valuative Criterion for Properness"
689	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
690	  % L2510-L2542). Original reference: EGA IV(2) 2.8.1, cited there.
691	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
692	  % "The functor $\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}$ satisfies the following valuative
693	  %  criterion for properness over $S$: given any discrete valuation ring
694	  %  $R$ over $S$ with quotient field $K$, the restriction map
695	  %  $$\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec R) \to \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec K)$$
696	  %  is bijective. This can be seen as follows.
697	  %  Given any coherent quotient
698	  %  $q: E_K \to \F$ on $X_R$ which defines an element $\langle \F, q\rangle$
699	  %  of $\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec K)$.
700	  %  Let $\ov{\F}$ be the image of the composite homomorphism
701	  %  $E_R \to j_*(E_K) \to j_*\F$ where $j : X_K \hra X_R$ is the open
702	  %  inclusion. Let $\ov{q} : E_R\to \ov{\F}$ be the induced surjection.
703	  %  Then we leave it to the reader to verify that
704	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ is an element of $\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec R)$
705	  %  which maps to $\langle \F, q\rangle$, and is the unique such element.
706	  %  (Use the basic fact that being flat over a dvr
707	  %  is the same as being torsion-free.)"
708	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, "Valuative Criterion for Properness"
709	  (FGA Explained Ch.~5); EGA IV(2) 2.8.1.}
710	  The functor $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ satisfies the valuative criterion
711	  for properness over $S$: for every discrete valuation ring $R$ with
712	  fraction field $K$ and every morphism $\Spec R \to S$, the restriction
713	  map
714	  \[
715	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}(\Spec R)
716	    \longrightarrow
717	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}(\Spec K)
718	  \]
719	  is bijective.
720	\end{lemma}
721	
722	\begin{proof}
723	  \uses{def:quot_functor}
724	  Given a $\Spec K$-point $\langle \mathcal{F}_K, q_K\rangle$ on $X_K$,
725	  let $j : X_K \hookrightarrow X_R$ be the open inclusion. Define
726	  $\overline{\mathcal{F}}$ as the image of the composite
727	  $E_R \to j_* E_K \to j_* \mathcal{F}_K$, with induced surjection
728	  $\overline{q} : E_R \to \overline{\mathcal{F}}$. The sheaf
729	  $\overline{\mathcal{F}}$ is torsion-free over $R$ (being a subsheaf of
730	  $j_* \mathcal{F}_K$), hence $R$-flat (flatness over a DVR
731	  $=$ torsion-freeness), and restricts on the generic fiber to
732	  $\mathcal{F}_K$. The pair $(\overline{\mathcal{F}}, \overline{q})$ is
733	  the unique extension of $\langle \mathcal{F}_K, q_K\rangle$ to a
734	  $\Spec R$-point (uniqueness: any other extension agrees on the dense
735	  open $X_K \subset X_R$ and is torsion-free, hence equals the image
736	  construction). The Hilbert polynomial is constant on connected
737	  $\Spec R$ by flatness.
738	\end{proof}
739	
740	\section{Cohomology and base change}
741	\label{sec:cohomology_base_change}
742	
743	The Quot construction uses cohomology-and-base-change in two places: the
744	boundedness step (\cref{lem:quot_boundedness}) and the Grassmannian
745	embedding (\cref{lem:quot_alpha_injective}). We record the relevant
746	statement as a named theorem so the Lean encoding can cite it directly.
747	
748	\begin{theorem}\leanok
749	[Flat base change of cohomology]
750	  \label{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}
751	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChangeCohomology}
752	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 02KH (lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology)
753	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L947-L970).
754	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
755	  % "Consider a cartesian diagram of schemes
756	  %  $$
757	  %  \xymatrix{
758	  %  X' \ar[d]_{f'} \ar[r]_{g'} & X \ar[d]^f \\
759	  %  S' \ar[r]^g & S
760	  %  }
761	  %  $$
762	  %  Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
763	  %  with pullback $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}$.
764	  %  Assume that $g$ is flat and that $f$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated.
765	  %  For any $i \geq 0$
766	  %  \begin{enumerate}
767	  %  \item the base change map of
768	  %  Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-base-change-map-flat-case}
769	  %  is an isomorphism
770	  %  $$
771	  %  g^*R^if_*\mathcal{F} \longrightarrow R^if'_*\mathcal{F}',
772	  %  $$
773	  %  \item if $S = \Spec(A)$ and $S' = \Spec(B)$, then
774	  %  $H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
775	  %  \end{enumerate}"
776	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 02KH (lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology).}
777	  Let
778	  \[
779	    \begin{array}{ccc}
780	      X' & \xrightarrow{g'} & X \\
781	      \downarrow f' &  & \downarrow f \\
782	      S' & \xrightarrow{g} & S
783	    \end{array}
784	  \]
785	  be a cartesian diagram of schemes with $g$ flat and $f$ quasi-compact and
786	  quasi-separated. Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent
787	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-module with pull-back
788	  $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^* \mathcal{F}$. For every $i \ge 0$:
789	  \begin{enumerate}
790	    \item the base-change map
791	      $g^* R^i f_* \mathcal{F} \to R^i f'_* \mathcal{F}'$ is an isomorphism;
792	    \item if $S = \Spec A$ and $S' = \Spec B$, then $H^i(X, \mathcal{F})
793	      \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
794	  \end{enumerate}
795	\end{theorem}
796	
797	\section{Lean encoding}
798	\label{sec:quot_lean_encoding}
799	
800	The Lean target file is the new
801	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean}. The construction
802	proceeds in three phases.
803	
804	\paragraph{Phase 1: Hilbert polynomial (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}).}
805	On a finite-type morphism $\pi : X \to S$ with $S$ noetherian and a
806	coherent $\mathcal{F}$ on $X$ with proper support over $S$, the per-fiber
807	Hilbert polynomial is encoded as a function $s \mapsto
808	\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]$. Mathlib provides
809	\texttt{CategoryTheory.PolynomialModule} and Euler-characteristic
810	infrastructure on coherent sheaves on noetherian schemes; the polynomial
811	property of $m \mapsto \chi(\mathcal{F} \otimes L^{\otimes m})$ requires
812	Snapper's Lemma, which is a project-side sub-build on top of the
813	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure.
814	
815	\paragraph{Phase 2: Grassmannian scheme (\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme},
816	\cref{thm:grassmannian_representable}).} The Grassmannian
817	$\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)$ is encoded as the relative gluing of $\binom{r}{d}$
818	affine charts $U^I$ (each isomorphic to a relative affine space
819	$\mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_S$) via the Pl\"ucker cocycle $\theta_{I,J}$. The
820	gluing backbone is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData}. The
821	universal quotient sheaf $\mathcal{U}$ is glued by the transition cocycle
822	$g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}$, and the determinant line bundle
823	$\det \mathcal{U}$ gives the Pl\"ucker closed embedding into
824	$\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)$. The representability statement uses
825	\texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy} (as in
826	\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}) on the universal pair
827	$(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d), \mathcal{U})$.
828	
829	\paragraph{Phase 3: Quot scheme (\cref{thm:quot_representable}).}
830	The Quot functor is constructed as a locally closed subfunctor of the
831	Grassmannian
832	$\mathrm{Grass}(W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V, \Phi(r))$, via:
833	\begin{enumerate}
834	  \item the uniform $m$-regularity bound from
835	    \cref{lem:quot_boundedness} (input: Castelnuovo--Mumford theorem,
836	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.CastelnuovoMumford} -- project-side sub-build);
837	  \item the Grassmannian embedding $\alpha$ from
838	    \cref{lem:quot_alpha_injective};
839	  \item the flattening stratification (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}
840	    of \cref{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification}, A.2.a) applied to the
841	    universal quotient on the Grassmannian, producing the locally closed
842	    stratum $T_0^\Phi$;
843	  \item the valuative-criterion upgrade
844	    (\cref{lem:quot_valuative_criterion}) to closed embedding, hence
845	    projective $S$-scheme.
846	\end{enumerate}
847	The reduction \cref{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W} from the universal
848	case to the general case is encoded as a closed embedding of representable
849	subfunctors via the kernel-vanishing scheme.
850	
851	\paragraph{Cohomology and base change.}
852	\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology} is the Mathlib-equivalent of
853	Stacks tag 02KH. Mathlib carries a partial form
854	(\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flat\_base\_change}) for affine bases; the
855	full $R^i f_*$ statement is a project-side bridge if not present at the
856	pinned commit.
857	
858	\paragraph{Dependencies summary.}
859	This file directly consumes
860	\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec} (A.1.a, on disk) for relative-spectrum
861	gluing infrastructure and
862	\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists} from
863	\cref{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification} (A.2.a, written this iter)
864	for the flattening stratum. Downstream, \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}
865	(A.2.c) consumes \cref{thm:quot_representable} via the special case
866	$E = \mathcal{O}_C$ on a smooth proper curve $C/k$, giving the Hilbert
867	scheme $\Hilb_{C/k} = \Quot^{\Phi,\mathcal{O}_C(1)}_{\mathcal{O}_C/C/k}$
868	and its open subscheme $\Div_{C/k}$ of relative effective divisors.
869	
870	\section{Out of scope}
871	\label{sec:quot_out_of_scope}
872	
873	This chapter packages \emph{only} the Quot-scheme existence statement and
874	its proof skeleton via the Grassmannian + flattening-stratification
875	route. The following are explicitly out of scope here:
876	\begin{itemize}
877	  \item \textbf{Flattening stratification} (A.2.a) -- consumed-by, not
878	    proved-here. Its blueprint home is the new chapter
879	    \texttt{Picard\_FlatteningStratification.tex}.
880	  \item \textbf{Hilbert scheme as a separate chapter}. The Hilbert scheme
881	    $\Hilb_{X/S}$ is the special case
882	    $\Hilb_{X/S} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}$ of the Quot scheme and is
883	    introduced inline (\cref{def:quot_functor}); no separate chapter.
884	  \item \textbf{Castelnuovo--Mumford $m$-regularity theorem} -- input to
885	    \cref{lem:quot_boundedness}, not proved here. The proof lives in
886	    Nitsure \S 2; for the Lean encoding it is a project-side sub-build.
887	  \item \textbf{Semi-continuity and base change} (Nitsure \S 3) -- the
888	    flat-base-change theorem of cohomology (\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology})
889	    is stated here as a lemma, but the upstream Mathlib bridge / project-side
890	    sub-build is not constructed in this chapter.
891	  \item \textbf{Snapper's Lemma} -- the polynomial-eventually property of
892	    Euler characteristics, used in \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. Cited
893	    via Nitsure \S 1 and (textbook reference) Hartshorne III.5.2;
894	    project-side sub-build for the Lean encoding.
895	  \item \textbf{Identification with $\mathbb{P}^n$ and relative
896	    Grassmannian variants} (Nitsure \S 1, Exercises (1)--(4)) --
897	    optional applications, not consumed by Route A.
898	  \item \textbf{Hironaka's counterexample to representability of
899	    $\Hilb_{X/\mathbb{C}}$ in the non-projective case} (Nitsure \S 1,
900	    Exercise (8)) -- pedagogical only; Route A operates entirely in the
901	    projective setting.
902	  \item \textbf{Group-scheme structure on $\Pic_{C/k}$} (A.2.c) -- the
903	    abelian-group-scheme refinement is downstream of the Quot existence
904	    statement; its blueprint home is
905	    \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}.
906	  \item \textbf{Identity-component $\Pic^0$ and abelian-variety
907	    structure} (A.3) -- a separate Route A sub-row, fed by but not
908	    constructed in this chapter.
909	\end{itemize}
910
